name: parallel-execution
description: Patterns for parallel subagent execution using Task tool with run_in_background. Use when coordinating multiple independent tasks, spawning dynamic subagents, or implementing features that can be parallelized.
capabilities: Spawn multiple subagents simultaneously using Task tool with run_in_background
Identify independent tasks suitable for parallel execution
Retrieve results from parallel tasks using TaskOutput
Synthesize results from multiple subagents into unified output
Apply directory-based and perspective-based parallelization patterns
author: CloudAI-X
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill with no executable code. The static analyzer's pattern detection generated false positives by misidentifying documentation elements (version strings, placeholders, examples) as security issues. Semantic analysis confirms: zero scripts, zero network calls, zero file system access, zero external commands. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Sequential task execution wastes time when independent tasks could run simultaneously. This skill teaches patterns for spawning multiple AI subagents concurrently using the Task tool, enabling dramatic speedups for multi-part projects.
userTitle: Run multiple AI subagents in parallel
limitations: All Task calls must be in a single assistant message for true parallelism
Tasks must be independent with no dependencies on each other
Cannot parallelize tasks that modify the same files
Does not work for sequential workflows requiring ordered steps

useCases.title: Implement feature modules
useCases.description: Build multiple independent features simultaneously, like auth, API endpoints, and database schema

useCases.title: Multi-perspective analysis
useCases.description: Run security, performance, testing, and architecture reviews concurrently

useCases.title: Parallel verification
useCases.description: Execute multiple independent checks and tests at the same time for faster feedback

promptTemplates.title: Basic parallel launch
promptTemplates.prompt:
I need to analyze this codebase from two perspectives. Launch both analyses in parallel using the Task tool with run_in_background: true.

Task 1: Analyze the security implementation
Task 2: Analyze the performance patterns

Then retrieve both results and summarize findings.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-module implementation
promptTemplates.prompt:
Implement three independent features for my project. Launch all three as parallel subagents:

- Subagent 1: User authentication module
- Subagent 2: API endpoints for user data
- Subagent 3: Database schema for users

Make all Task calls in one message. After completion, combine into cohesive implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Directory-based parallel
promptTemplates.prompt:
Analyze three directories in parallel: src/auth, src/api, src/db.

For each, review: code quality, security issues, test coverage.
Launch three subagents simultaneously. Synthesize findings by severity.

promptTemplates.title: Perspective review
promptTemplates.prompt:
Perform a comprehensive code review using four parallel subagents:

- Security reviewer: Check for vulnerabilities
- Performance reviewer: Identify bottlenecks
- Testing reviewer: Evaluate test coverage
- Architecture reviewer: Assess design patterns

All in one message. Combine recommendations with priority ordering.

bestPractices: Launch all parallel tasks in a single assistant message to ensure true concurrency
Verify tasks are independent before parallelizing - check for file and output dependencies
Use descriptive task descriptions to easily track which subagent handles what
antiPatterns: Splitting Task calls across multiple messages (causes sequential execution)
Parallelizing tasks that depend on each other's output
Running multiple tasks that modify the same files (causes conflicts)

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude Code and Claude. Requires Task tool with run_in_background support.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of parallel tasks?
faq.answer: The limit depends on your Claude Code plan. Generally 5-10 parallel tasks work well.

faq.question: How do I integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Use TodoWrite to track parallel tasks. After TaskOutput, pass results to orchestrator skills.

faq.question: Is my data safe with parallel execution?
faq.answer: Yes. All processing happens within your Claude Code session. No external data transmission.

faq.question: Why are my tasks running sequentially?
faq.answer: Common cause: Task calls are in separate messages. All Task calls MUST be in one message.

faq.question: How does this compare to sequential execution?
faq.answer: Parallel execution can be Nx faster. Five 30-second tasks complete in ~30 seconds instead of ~150 seconds.
